skill.name: longbridge-value-investing
skill.description: Value investing research skill for Graham NCAV analysis, Graham screening, Buffett moat diagnostics, and Buffett-style stock screening using Longbridge data.
skill.summary: Analyze stocks with Graham deep-value and Buffett moat frameworks.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command and weak-crypto patterns. Manual review found no prompt-injection attempt or malicious code; weak-crypto hits are false positives, while Longbridge CLI execution and WebSearch fallback are intentional workflow features. Publish with a medium-risk warning because user-influenced market symbols are passed to local CLI commands and live searches.

medium_findings.title: Assistant-Directed Longbridge CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The static external-command findings are true positives in context. The skill instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands, often with user-selected symbols, indexes, or sectors. The commands are limited to Longbridge data retrieval and include symbol-resolution guidance, so this is not confirmed malware, but inputs should be validated before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly define Longbridge CLI invocations and error handling. Risk is moderated because the commands target a legitimate financial-data CLI and use structured placeholders rather than arbitrary shell snippets.

low_findings.title: Live WebSearch Fallback for Market Data Gaps
low_findings.description: The skill permits WebSearch when Longbridge lacks data such as industry outlook, PMI, capacity use, regulatory news, or Berkshire holdings. This creates normal network and data-provenance risk, but the files require source labels and restrict WebSearch to genuine data gaps.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple references explicitly permit WebSearch and require source attribution. I found no evidence that searches send credentials or private data to an external endpoint.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Weak-Crypto and Markdown Backtick Hits
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts appear to be false positives from Markdown filenames, headings, finance terms, and example formatting. The backtick alerts in SKILL.md include inline command names, related skill names, and a file layout block rather than Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Markdown prompt instructions and financial-analysis text, not cryptographic APIs or Ruby backtick operators. No executable source files were present in the scanned file set.

dangerous_patterns.title: Assistant-Directed Longbridge CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static external-command findings are true positives in context. The skill instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands, often with user-selected symbols, indexes, or sectors. The commands are limited to Longbridge data retrieval and include symbol-resolution guidance, so this is not confirmed malware, but inputs should be validated before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly define Longbridge CLI invocations and error handling. Risk is moderated because the commands target a legitimate financial-data CLI and use structured placeholders rather than arbitrary shell snippets.

content.user_title: Analyze Value Stocks with Graham and Buffett
content.value_statement: Value investors need repeatable checks for cheap stocks, quality businesses, and data consistency. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Graham and Buffett research workflows using Longbridge data.
actual_capabilities: Runs Graham single-stock NCAV diagnostics with value-trap checks.
Screens market or index universes for Graham-style deep-value candidates.
Builds Buffett moat diagnostics across business, finance, management, valuation, and visibility.
Screens for Buffett-style quality compounders using hard filters and moat scoring.
Requires data-source appendices and cross-statement reconciliation before scoring.
Supports Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English responses.
limitations: It does not provide personalized investment advice or buy and sell recommendations.
Results depend on Longbridge CLI or MCP data availability and command behavior.
WebSearch is only a fallback for data that Longbridge does not provide.
Some sectors need special handling because standard Graham or Buffett metrics may not fit.

use_cases.title: Review a Deep-Value Stock
use_cases.description: Assess one ticker with Graham NCAV, debt, dividend, earnings, and value-trap checks.

use_cases.title: Build a Graham Watchlist
use_cases.description: Screen an index or watchlist for low-valuation candidates with holding-period context.

use_cases.title: Compare Quality Compounders
use_cases.description: Find Buffett-style companies with strong returns, cleaner balance sheets, and visible moats.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Stock
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Graham framework to analyze 00700.HK. Show the NCAV score, value-trap checks, and disclaimer.

prompt_templates.title: Screen an Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen the HSI universe for Graham-style deep-value candidates. Return the top names with data limitations and waiting-cost notes.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Moat Diagnostic
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze AAPL.US with the Buffett moat framework. Include moat width, financial health, valuation, and minimum holding period.

prompt_templates.title: Customize a Quality Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Find US consumer stocks with ROE above 15%, positive free cash flow, and durable pricing power. Explain each exclusion and data source.

output_examples.input: Analyze a single Hong Kong stock with Graham rules.
output_examples.output: A structured report with NCAV ratio, static score, dynamic adjustments, liquidation-value table, holding-period view, and a source appendix.

output_examples.input: Find Buffett-style names in a sector.
output_examples.output: Three to five candidate cards with moat type, quality stars, valuation read, minimum holding period, and follow-up diagnostic prompts.

output_examples.input: Screen an index for cigar-butt candidates.
output_examples.output: A ranked table with Graham buy lines, value-trap warnings, excluded sectors, market summary, and investment-advice disclaimer.

best_practices: Provide exact ticker, market, index, or sector constraints before screening.
Review the data-source appendix before relying on any score or ranking.
Use single-stock diagnostics after a batch screen identifies candidates.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the score as a direct buy or sell recommendation.
Do not use Graham NCAV rules for banks, insurers, REITs, or financial shells.
Do not mix WebSearch data with Longbridge figures without clear source labels.

faq.question: Does this skill give investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides research structure and metric interpretation for informational use only.

faq.question: What data source does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers Longbridge CLI data, then MCP fallback, and WebSearch only for genuine gaps.

faq.question: Can it screen a full market?
faq.answer: It can screen an index, sector, or watchlist, with batch limits described in the references.

faq.question: How does it handle bad or inconsistent data?
faq.answer: It requires reconciliation checks and may halt scoring or drop rows when gaps exceed tolerance.

faq.question: Which languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English output.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally uses local Longbridge CLI commands and live WebSearch, so inputs and sources need review.
